description: Introduce a typed request-scoped relational authorization context for GET-many and pass it through the relational query request path. Acceptance: `RelationalQueryRequest`/`IRelationalQueryRequest` exposes a backend-facing context built from `RequestInfo.ClientAuthorizations`; the context contains `ClaimEducationOrganizationIds` as unique `long` values sorted ascending; the context supports an empty EdOrg list without throwing; relational GET-many authorization code no longer parses EdOrg IDs from `AuthorizationStrategyEvaluator.Filters`; the logical auth-list parameter name is centralized as `ClaimEducationOrganizationIds`.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests proving duplicate EdOrg claims are deduplicated, sorted ascending, and preserved as an empty list when no EdOrg claims exist.
Add a unit test proving relational query request creation carries the typed context alongside existing query elements, pagination, profile context, and strategy evaluators.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Add the strategy names and provider behavior needed for DMS-1055 staging. Acceptance: constants exist for `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted`, `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeopleInverted`, `RelationshipsWithPeopleOnly`, and `OwnershipBased`; the shared authorization registry recognizes `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted`; GET-by-id, POST, PUT, and DELETE with `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted` fail intentionally as not implemented until DMS-1056 instead of falling through to missing-provider 403 behavior; existing strategy names keep their current behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for `NamedAuthorizationServiceFactory` or the relevant middleware proving the inverted EdOrg-only strategy is recognized.
Add unit tests for non-GET operations showing `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted` produces an explicit not-implemented failure surface rather than a missing provider 403.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Refactor `ProvideAuthorizationFiltersMiddleware` so relational GET-many reaches the backend planner with raw strategy names and typed claim context. Acceptance: in the relational query pipeline, empty EdOrg claim lists do not produce the current relationship-provider 403; known-but-not-implemented strategies are allowed through for backend classification; convention-shaped custom view strategy names are allowed through for backend classification; truly missing legacy providers still produce the existing response outside relational GET-many; legacy backend and non-query paths retain existing filter-provider behavior.
steps-to-verify: Add middleware tests for relational GET-many with empty EdOrg claims, `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted`, `NamespaceBased`, `OwnershipBased`, a People relationship strategy, and a `{BasisResource}With...` custom view name.
Add regression tests proving the legacy backend or non-query paths still use existing provider validation and missing-provider failure behavior.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Implement a relational GET-many authorization strategy classifier for DMS-1055. Acceptance: `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly` and `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted` are classified as supported relationship strategies; `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` is classified as a no-op and ignored when combined with relationship strategies; `NamespaceBased`, `OwnershipBased`, `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeople`, `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeopleInverted`, `RelationshipsWithPeopleOnly`, `RelationshipsWithStudentsOnly`, `RelationshipsWithStudentsOnlyThroughResponsibility`, and custom view strategy names matching `{BasisResource}With...` with a basis resource or descriptor resolvable from the effective schema are classified as known-but-not-implemented and produce 501 for DMS-1055; convention-matching custom view names resolve the basis resource using effective-schema lookup that prefers standard `edfi` resources over extension homographs and also accepts descriptor bases; non-matching custom names, custom names with an unknown basis resource, and unknown built-in names produce the security-configuration 500 path; classification runs before empty-claim short-circuiting so unsupported mixed strategies still return 501.
steps-to-verify: Add backend unit tests for supported EdOrg-only normal/inverted strategies, normal plus inverted OR grouping, `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` as a no-op, and unsupported mixed strategy 501 classification.

Add backend unit tests for custom view name classification: standard resource preferred, descriptor basis accepted, unknown basis rejected as security configuration, and non-convention names rejected as security configuration.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Add the canonical request-time security-configuration failure surface for relational GET-many authorization metadata errors. Acceptance: query results can represent a security configuration failure separately from generic `UnknownFailure`; `QueryRequestHandler` maps it to HTTP 500 with `Content-Type: application/problem+json`; the body uses `type: urn:ed-fi:api:system-configuration:security`, `title: Security Configuration Error`, `detail: A security configuration problem was detected. The request cannot be authorized.`, `status: 500`, `correlationId`, and an `errors` array carrying actionable diagnostics such as the affected resource, strategy name, and unresolved securable path or basis-resource details; normal GET-many authorization denial still returns 200 with filtered results; known unsupported DMS-1055 strategies still return 501.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for the new `FailureResponse` helper or equivalent ProblemDetails mapping.
Add `QueryRequestHandler` tests for security configuration 500, known unsupported 501, and normal empty successful pages.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Select DMS-1055 EdOrg authorization subjects from resolved securable paths using ODS-parity root/base scope. Acceptance: resolved securable path metadata carries enough diagnostics to report the original JSON path and readable/MetaEd name for EdOrg subjects; supported EdOrg-only GET-many authorization uses only `EducationOrganization` paths whose resolved column is on the aggregate root table, or the base table for derived resources; child collection EdOrg paths remain resolved and indexed but are excluded from DMS-1055 GET-many subjects; documents with a valid root/base EdOrg subject are not excluded just because a configured child collection is empty; when a securable element has multiple candidate root/base paths, the valid shortest/canonical root/base path is used and an unresolvable sibling path does not hide it; multiple root/base EdOrg subjects within one strategy are retained and later combined with AND; a strategy with no applicable root/base EdOrg subjects fails as a security configuration error with resource, strategy, and path details; generated authorization SQL does not join child collection tables for EdOrg-only relationship GET-many.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for a resource with one root EdOrg subject, a derived-resource base-table EdOrg subject, multiple root/base EdOrg subjects, both root and child EdOrg paths, root-plus-child paths where the child collection is empty, child-only EdOrg paths, and mixed valid/unresolvable root/base candidate paths where the valid shortest/canonical path still wins.
Add a regression test proving child-only paths fail as security configuration rather than returning all rows or an empty page.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj`
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Fix DMS-1055 root/base EdOrg subject selection when multiple configured securable elements share the same `metaEdName`. Issue: `RelationalEdOrgAuthorizationSubjectSelector` currently collapses candidates by readable name, so real DS 5.2 resources such as `CourseOffering`, `SectionAttendanceTakenEvent`, and `StudentSchoolAttendanceEvent` can lose one of two distinct root/base `SchoolId` subjects and authorize on only one path instead of ANDing both. Acceptance: subject selection keys the keep/drop decision by configured securable element/path rather than only `metaEdName`; if two distinct root/base EdOrg paths with the same name both resolve, both subjects are retained and later combined with AND; the existing DMS-1055 root-plus-child behavior still keeps only the applicable root/base subject when a child-table path shares that same name; an unresolved sibling path still does not hide a valid shortest/canonical root/base candidate for the same configured element.
steps-to-verify: Add subject-selector unit coverage for two distinct root/base EdOrg securable elements that share one `metaEdName`, proving both subjects are retained, plus a regression proving root-plus-child same-name duplicates still keep only the root/base subject.

Add repository or integration regression coverage for a real-authoritative or focused fixture resource with duplicate root/base `SchoolId` subjects so GET-many requires authorization on both paths rather than only one.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj`
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Authorization`

description: Cache only the helper results needed to build DMS-1055 authorization predicates, not the generated authorization SQL itself. Acceptance: resolved EdOrg securable path or eligible root/base-subject calculations are cached by effective schema or mapping-set identity, resource, and securable element; repeated relational GET-many planning for the same key reuses the cached helper result; different schema hashes, resources, or securable elements do not reuse the wrong entry; generated authorization SQL and strategy composition remain request-built and are not stored in compiled/AOT plan artifacts or a resource-level SQL cache.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests around the helper cache proving the same schema/resource/securable-element key reuses a resolved result while different keys trigger independent resolution.
Add a regression test proving authorization SQL still reflects the current strategy set and parameter shape instead of reusing a cached resource-specific SQL string.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Extend page DocumentId query planning/SQL generation with EdOrg relationship authorization predicates. Acceptance: the page query and optional total-count query apply the same authorization filter; `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly` filters `auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId.SourceEducationOrganizationId` by claim EdOrg IDs and compares `TargetEducationOrganizationId` to the root/base resource EdOrg column; `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted` swaps Source/Target; multiple subjects inside one strategy are combined with AND; multiple relationship strategies are wrapped and combined with OR; `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` contributes no SQL; SQL uses an IN subquery or equivalent predicate rather than joining auth rows to the page query, so duplicate auth rows or matching strategies do not duplicate documents; existing query predicates, descriptor predicates, paging, and total count remain compatible.
steps-to-verify: Add SQL compiler/unit tests for PostgreSQL and SQL Server normal strategy SQL, inverted strategy SQL, normal-plus-inverted OR SQL, multi-subject AND SQL, `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` no-op behavior, and matching authorization predicates on both page and total-count SQL.
Add SQL shape assertions proving child collection tables are absent and auth predicates bind to the aggregate root alias `r` or base alias `b` for derived resources.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Implement dialect-specific claim EdOrg ID SQL parameterization for the authorization predicate. Acceptance: this parameterization path is used only when the deduplicated claim list is non-empty because repository integration short-circuits empty claim sets before SQL generation; PostgreSQL uses one `bigint[]` parameter named `@ClaimEducationOrganizationIds` and SQL uses `= ANY(@ClaimEducationOrganizationIds)`; SQL Server uses scalar `bigint` parameters named `@ClaimEducationOrganizationIds_0`, `@ClaimEducationOrganizationIds_1`, etc. when the deduplicated claim list has fewer than 2,000 entries; SQL Server uses one structured parameter named `@ClaimEducationOrganizationIds` and SQL references `SELECT [Id] FROM @ClaimEducationOrganizationIds` or an equivalent subquery when the deduplicated claim list has 2,000 or more entries; IDs are deduplicated before threshold selection and sorted ascending for deterministic parameter names, logs, tests, and TVP row order; token IDs are never inlined into SQL.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests for 1, 1,999, 2,000, and duplicate-heavy claim lists in both dialects.
Add SQL assertions proving PostgreSQL uses a single array parameter and SQL Server switches from scalar parameters to the TVP shape at exactly 2,000 unique IDs.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Extend the query/runtime parameter model and binders so authorization parameters can carry provider-specific metadata without putting provider delegates in compiled/AOT plan contracts. Acceptance: query parameter contracts can describe normal scalar values, PostgreSQL array values, and SQL Server structured TVP values; `HydrationBatchBuilder` or its runtime companion binds the new parameter shapes for both page and total-count SQL; SQL Server TVP binding creates a `Microsoft.Data.SqlClient.SqlParameter` with `ParameterName = @ClaimEducationOrganizationIds`, `SqlDbType = Structured`, `TypeName = dms.BigIntTable`, and a value table with exactly one `long` column named `Id`; existing scalar query/paging parameter binding continues to work.
steps-to-verify: Add unit tests around parameter inventory/value validation for scalar, array, and TVP parameters, including parameters shared by page and total-count SQL.
Add SQL Server binder tests proving the structured parameter has `SqlDbType.Structured`, `TypeName = dms.BigIntTable`, and an `Id` column with sorted unique values.
Add PostgreSQL binder tests proving the array parameter is bound as a single value under `@ClaimEducationOrganizationIds`.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj`

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Mssql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~RelationalQuery`

description: Wire DMS-1055 authorization into `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.QueryDocuments`. Acceptance: relational GET-many gets query capability and read plan as before, classifies authorization strategies, returns 501 for known unsupported strategies before checking empty claims, returns the canonical security-configuration 500 for invalid strategy metadata or no applicable root/base EdOrg subjects, returns `QuerySuccess([], 0)` when supported EdOrg relationship authorization applies and `ClaimEducationOrganizationIds` is empty with `totalCount=true`, returns an empty array with null total count when `totalCount=false`, and otherwise passes the authorization predicate/parameters into page keyset planning and hydration; existing descriptor query behavior and `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired`-only behavior remain unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Add repository unit tests for supported normal filtering path, supported inverted path, unsupported mixed strategy 501, unknown strategy security configuration 500, empty EdOrg claim short-circuit with and without totalCount, and `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired`-only no-op behavior.
Add regression tests proving existing query filters and profile readable projection still flow into hydration when authorization is active.
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj`

description: Add PostgreSQL backend integration coverage for EdOrg-only relationship GET-many authorization. Acceptance: tests run against generated relational DDL with `auth.EducationOrganizationIdToEducationOrganizationId`; normal strategy returns only resources whose root/base EdOrg is reachable from the token EdOrg; inverted strategy swaps Source/Target and returns bottom-to-top authorized rows; normal plus inverted strategies are ORed; multiple root/base EdOrg subjects in one strategy are ANDed; duplicate auth rows or two matching strategies return one document once; paging and `Total-Count` are calculated after authorization filtering; empty EdOrg claims return an empty page and total count 0; derived resources authorize through the base-table EdOrg subject; root-plus-child EdOrg resources authorize from the root/base subject only, including when the child collection is empty; child-only EdOrg subject resources fail as security configuration.
steps-to-verify: Create focused PostgreSQL integration tests with small fixture data for normal, inverted, OR, AND, duplicate avoidance, paging/count, empty claims, derived-resource base-table authorization, root-plus-child including an empty-child variant, and child-only configuration error cases.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Postgresql.Tests.Integration.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Authorization`

description: Add SQL Server backend integration coverage for EdOrg-only relationship GET-many authorization and threshold-specific binding. Acceptance: SQL Server behavior matches the PostgreSQL authorization cases for normal, inverted, OR, AND, duplicate avoidance, paging/count, empty claims, derived-resource base-table authorization, root-plus-child including an empty-child variant, and child-only configuration failures; 1,999 unique claim EdOrg IDs use expanded scalar parameters; 2,000 unique claim EdOrg IDs use the `dms.BigIntTable` TVP; duplicate token IDs are deduped before choosing the threshold; structured parameter execution succeeds against the emitted `dms.BigIntTable` type.
steps-to-verify: Create focused SQL Server integration tests for the shared authorization behavior, including derived-resource and empty-child root/base cases, and separate tests for 1,999, 2,000, and duplicate-heavy claim lists.

description: Run final formatting and the focused regression suite for DMS-1055. Acceptance: all touched C# files are formatted with CSharpier; unit tests for Core, Backend, and Plans pass; focused PostgreSQL and SQL Server integration tests pass; focused relational-backend E2E authorization scenarios pass.
steps-to-verify: Run: `dotnet csharpier format ./src/dms`

description: High: Preserve distinct root/base EdOrg authorization subjects when multiple configured securable elements share the same `metaEdName`. Issue: `RelationalEdOrgAuthorizationSubjectSelector` currently groups resolved and unresolved EdOrg elements by readable name / `MetaEdName`, so resources with two different root/base `SchoolId` subjects such as `CourseOffering` (`$.schoolReference.schoolId` and `$.sessionReference.schoolId`), `SectionAttendanceTakenEvent`, and `StudentSchoolAttendanceEvent` collapse to a single authorization predicate. This weakens the required DMS-1055 AND semantics inside one relationship strategy and can return rows when the caller is authorized for only one participating EdOrg subject. Acceptance: subject selection keys off configured securable-element identity (at least `jsonPath`) instead of only `MetaEdName`; distinct root/base EdOrg elements with the same readable name are all retained and later ANDed within a single strategy; an unresolved same-name element still raises a security-configuration failure when it is a distinct configured subject rather than an alternate candidate path for the same configured `jsonPath`; focused tests cover real same-name cases and prevent regressions for both normal and inverted EdOrg-only strategies.
steps-to-verify: Add `RelationalEdOrgAuthorizationSubjectSelector` tests proving two distinct root/base EdOrg securable elements with the same `metaEdName` are both selected, and that a distinct unresolved same-name subject still fails as a security configuration error.

Add backend query-authorization tests for a real same-name resource shape such as `CourseOffering`, asserting GET-many requires authorization on both root/base `SchoolId` subjects for `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly` and `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted`.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~RelationalEdOrgAuthorizationSubjectSelectorTests`
Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~Authorization`

description: High: Return the canonical security-configuration 500 when a relational GET-many request matches a resource claim/action whose authorization-strategy list is empty, and align all DMS security-configuration ProblemDetails responses with the ODS-parity URN. Issue: `ResourceActionAuthorizationMiddleware.ValidateAuthorizationStrategies` still turns that misconfiguration into a 403 Authorization Denied response, which hides incomplete security metadata as an access denial; the current DMS canonical helper/tests also use a non-ODS hyphenated system-configuration type. Acceptance: DMS uses `type: urn:ed-fi:api:system:configuration:security` for every canonical security-configuration ProblemDetails response; for the DMS-1055 relational GET-many lane, a matched claim/action with zero authorization strategies returns HTTP 500 with `Content-Type: application/problem+json`, `title: Security Configuration Error`, `detail: A security configuration problem was detected. The request cannot be authorized.`, the request correlationId, and an `errors` entry stating that no authorization strategies were defined for the matched claim/resource action; explicit `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` remains a valid strategy that reaches the backend; true access denials caused by no matching claim or no matching action remain 403.
steps-to-verify: Update `FailureResponse.ForSecurityConfiguration` and existing handler/response tests to assert the ODS-parity `urn:ed-fi:api:system:configuration:security` type instead of the current hyphenated system-configuration type.

Add middleware or request-pipeline unit tests for a relational GET-many request whose matched claim/action has zero authorization strategies, asserting the canonical 500 security-configuration ProblemDetails body and error text.
Add regression tests proving relational GET-many still proceeds when the matched action uses `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired`, and that missing-claim or missing-action denials still return 403.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~ResourceActionAuthorizationMiddlewareTests`

description: Medium: Parse `{BasisResource}With...` custom-view strategy names by matching the full effective-schema resource/descriptor prefix, not the first `With` token. Issue: `RelationalGetManyAuthorizationStrategyClassifier.ResolveCustomViewStrategy` currently splits at the first `With`, so real basis names that contain `With` such as `ExitWithdrawTypeDescriptor` are truncated to `Exit` and incorrectly surface the security-configuration 500 path instead of DMS-1055's known-but-not-implemented 501 classification. Acceptance: custom-view basis resolution finds a valid effective-schema resource or descriptor whose full name is the longest prefix immediately followed by `With`; standard `Ed-Fi` homographs still win over extensions; `ExitWithdrawTypeDescriptorWithSomething` and similar names classify as known-but-not-implemented custom-view strategies with the correct full basis resource; names that do not have any valid basis-resource prefix still return the existing security-configuration failure.
steps-to-verify: Add `RelationalGetManyAuthorizationStrategyClassifier` tests proving strategy names like `ExitWithdrawTypeDescriptorWithSomething` resolve to the full descriptor/resource basis instead of truncating at the first `With`.

Add regression tests proving malformed names with no valid basis-resource prefix still return the security-configuration failure path, and that standard `Ed-Fi` homographs remain preferred over extensions.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~RelationalGetManyAuthorizationStrategyClassifier`

description: Medium: Fix invalid custom-view strategy names that only match a real basis resource by delimiter position or name length. Issue: `RelationalGetManyAuthorizationStrategyClassifier.ResolveCustomViewStrategy` checks the position of `With` but does not verify the strategy name starts with the candidate `resource.ResourceName`, so names such as `PotatoWithSomething` can resolve as a same-length basis resource like `School` and return DMS-1055's known-but-not-implemented 501 instead of the required security-configuration 500. Acceptance: custom-view classification accepts only effective-schema resources or descriptors whose full resource name is an ordinal prefix immediately followed by `With`; malformed names whose prefix is not an effective-schema resource or descriptor produce the canonical security-configuration failure; valid custom-view names still classify as known-but-not-implemented 501; the earlier longest-prefix behavior for real basis names containing `With` remains intact.
steps-to-verify: Update `RelationalGetManyAuthorizationStrategyClassifier.ResolveCustomViewStrategy` to require `strategyName.StartsWith(resource.ResourceName + "With", StringComparison.Ordinal)` or an equivalent ordinal full-prefix parse before accepting a resource or descriptor basis; do not accept a candidate solely because `With` occurs at `resource.ResourceName.Length`.

Add a regression test where the bad basis prefix has the same character length as a real resource, such as `PotatoWithSomething` or `BogussWithSomething` versus `School`, and assert the security-configuration 500 classification path.

Add or retain tests proving a valid `{BasisResource}With...` strategy still returns the known-but-not-implemented 501 classification and that basis names containing `With` still resolve to the full basis name.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~RelationalGetManyAuthorizationStrategyClassifier`

description: Medium: Correct the readable-profile authorization regression test expectation for `_etag`. Issue: `RelationalDocumentStoreRepositoryTests` around the readable-profile query result path appears to expect projected JSON to recompute `_etag`, but the production query projection path returns the projected JSON unchanged and should not synthesize or recompute `_etag` for authorized GET-many results. Acceptance: the test asserts the repository returns the exact projected JSON produced by the projection path, including the unchanged `_etag` value or absence of `_etag`; no production change is made solely to satisfy a recomputed `_etag` expectation; existing readable-profile filtering and authorization behavior remain covered.
steps-to-verify: Update the new test near `RelationalDocumentStoreRepositoryTests.cs:1777` so its fixture data and assertions match unchanged projected JSON rather than a recomputed `_etag`.

Add an assertion that distinguishes this from recomputation, such as preserving a deliberately non-recomputed projected `_etag` sentinel value and/or preserving the projected document instance exactly.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDocumentStoreRepositoryTests`

description: Low: Strengthen `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyRelational.feature` coverage so the normal-plus-inverted scenario actually requires inverted access. Issue: the scenario around line 42 uses token EdOrg 2, which already authorizes `schoolId=20101` through the normal top-down `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnly` strategy, so a normal-only implementation can pass while inverted OR behavior is broken. Acceptance: the feature uses a row/token/document combination where at least one expected document is not reachable through the normal strategy and is reachable through `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyInverted`, or splits the table into explicit normal-only and inverted-only cases; a normal-only implementation would fail the inverted case; scenario names and expected rows make the authorization direction clear.
steps-to-verify: Revise `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Authorization/RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyRelational.feature` so at least one row can only pass through inverted Source/Target authorization; avoid using token EdOrg 2 with `schoolId=20101` as the only OR proof because that is already authorized top-down.
If a single scenario table becomes ambiguous, split it into separate normal-only and inverted-only scenarios with distinct token EdOrg and resource EdOrg combinations.
Run from `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E`: `pwsh ./teardown-local-dms.ps1` then `pwsh ./setup-local-dms.ps1`.

description: Low: Remove dead `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` surface from the lower-level page-document authorization contract after confirming it has no remaining runtime responsibility. Issue: the strategy classifier consumes `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` as a no-op before repository mapping, and `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository` only maps relationship strategies into page authorization specs, leaving `PageDocumentIdQueryModels` and compiler normalization support for no-further-auth as unused runtime surface. Acceptance: `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` handling stays centralized before page planning; page-document query models represent only authorization predicates that can affect generated SQL; compiler and normalization code no longer carry a no-op page authorization branch; repository/classifier tests still prove `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` alone grants no-filter GET-many access after claim/action authorization and mixed relationship cases continue to apply only the relationship predicates.
steps-to-verify: Confirm `NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired` is no longer needed by lower-level page-document compilation, then remove the unused model member from `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/PageDocumentIdQueryModels.cs` and any compiler normalization branches that only support that no-op member.
Update affected plan compiler tests to assert absence of no-op authorization SQL, and keep classifier/repository tests for the actual no-further-auth behavior.

description: Low: De-duplicate exact physical EdOrg authorization subjects at the SQL-subject boundary. Issue: duplicate selected subjects that resolve to the same physical `(Table, Column)` can produce redundant AND subqueries, while distinct logical references that land on different physical columns must remain separate. Acceptance: `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.QueryDocuments` maps selected EdOrg subjects to `PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSubject` values and applies `DistinctBy(static subject => (subject.Table, subject.Column))`; de-duplication is not moved into metadata subject selection and is not based on `ReadableName`, `JsonPath`, or `MetaEdName`; normal and inverted strategies remain separate OR branches and are not deduplicated away; duplicate physical subjects compile to one authorization predicate per strategy, while two different columns still compile to two predicates.
steps-to-verify: Update the authorization subject mapping in `RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.cs` around the `selectedEdOrgSubjects.Subjects.Select(...)` projection to de-duplicate only by physical `(Table, Column)` after metadata selection has completed.
Add a focused regression test where duplicate selected subjects with the same table and column compile to one authorization subject predicate per strategy.
Add a companion regression test where two selected subjects with different physical columns still compile to two authorization predicates combined with AND inside a strategy.

Run: `dotnet test --no-restore ./src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~RelationalDocumentStoreRepositoryTests`

description: Low: Apply the remaining code-review readability fixes for DMS-1055 relational authorization. Issue: `HydrationBatchBuilder.TryGetRequiredParameter` uses `FirstOrDefault(...) ?? null!`, which hides the nullable intent; `RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyRelational.feature` uses broad authorization to seed data before switching to the narrow claim set under test, which makes scenario intent harder to read; the same feature uses lowercase `#school` descriptor values even though these scenarios are not intended to test descriptor case-insensitivity. Acceptance: `TryGetRequiredParameter` removes the null-forgiving coalesce by declaring the out value nullable, preferably with `[NotNullWhen(true)]`, and returns `parameter is not null`; the relational EdOrg-only E2E setup distinguishes seed/admin context from the query authorization context or otherwise makes the broad setup auth explicit without changing tested behavior; school category descriptors in this feature use canonical `#School` casing unless a scenario is explicitly about descriptor case-insensitive behavior.
steps-to-verify: Update `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/HydrationBatchBuilder.cs` so `TryGetRequiredParameter` has nullable-aware output instead of `?? null!`, and add `System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis` only if needed for `[NotNullWhen(true)]`.

Revise `src/dms/tests/EdFi.DataManagementService.Tests.E2E/Features/Authorization/RelationshipsWithEdOrgsOnlyRelational.feature` so setup authorization and query authorization are clearly separated in the scenario text or implemented through a setup-only/admin seeding step.
Align the `EducationOrganizationCategoryDescriptor` school values in that feature to canonical `uri://tpdm.ed-fi.org/EducationOrganizationCategoryDescriptor#School` casing.
